meta.purpose: Encode world-class judgment for programmatic video animation with Motion Canvas: scenes, signals, layout, camera rhythm, explainers, and code maintainability.
meta.load_condition: Load when the task involves Motion Canvas, programmatic video generation, technical explainer videos, or converting code architecture into visual animations.

axioms.one_sentence: A Motion Canvas scene should express one argument or one visual transformation.
axioms.full_statement: A Motion Canvas scene should express one argument or one visual transformation.
axioms.why: Overloaded scenes become hard to read, review, and reuse.
applies_when: Animating a single step of a sorting algorithm
Transitioning a graph from a tree to a linear chain structure
Introducing a new variable's role before combining it with others
does_not_apply_when: Creating a rapid stylistic montage to match upbeat background music
Simulating a live desktop environment where multiple applications run concurrently
Establishing a complex static background that intentionally layers several unrelated props
axioms.failure_risk: The agent will over-fragment cohesive explanations into repetitive, disjointed clips that destroy narrative pacing and viewer engagement.

axioms.one_sentence: Animation code must remain readable as a storyboard.
axioms.full_statement: Animation code must remain readable as a storyboard.
axioms.why: If timing logic is scattered, the video cannot be directed.
applies_when: Sequencing camera movements to align with specific voiceover timestamps
Structuring play sequences so scene progression matches a written script
Organizing keyframe and transition blocks in chronological order
does_not_apply_when: Writing a physics engine or mathematical simulation where frame-independent calculations prioritize performance
Developing a reusable utility library or custom component shared across unrelated videos
Auto-generating procedural textures or meshes where the code describes generation rules rather than narrative beats
axioms.failure_risk: The agent will force rigidly sequential and verbose structures onto performance-critical simulations or reusable libraries, degrading execution speed and code maintainability.

axioms.one_sentence: Technical animations should prove an idea visually, not merely decorate narration.
axioms.full_statement: Technical animations should prove an idea visually, not merely decorate narration.
axioms.why: The viewer should understand the logic even with the sound muted.
applies_when: Demonstrating how a cryptographic hash maps variable inputs to fixed outputs
Tracing data packets through a network routing table step by step
Visualizing the mechanical interaction between interlocking gears
does_not_apply_when: Producing ambient B-roll for a philosophical monologue that relies on abstract metaphor
Creating a product launch teaser where emotional resonance and brand tone take priority
Generating decorative lower-thirds or notification badges to accompany a live presenter
axioms.failure_risk: The agent will over-engineer literal visual demonstrations for rhetorical or emotional segments, producing cluttered, distracting graphics that obscure the intended message.

axioms.one_sentence: Repeated visual motifs create cognitive continuity.
axioms.full_statement: Repeated visual motifs create cognitive continuity.
axioms.why: Explainers become memorable when transformations reuse visual grammar.
applies_when: Creating multi-part tutorial series where consistent iconography tracks recurring concepts across episodes
Building brand explainers that reuse logo geometry or color palettes during scene transitions
Designing software walkthroughs where identical hover and click animations apply to different interface modules
does_not_apply_when: Producing fast-paced meme compilations where rapid stylistic shifts are required to maintain comedic timing
Visualizing highly distinct categorical datasets that require unique shapes and colors to prevent pattern confusion
Crafting experimental or surrealist animations where intentional visual fragmentation supports an abstract narrative
axioms.failure_risk: The agent forces repetitive visual elements into disparate contexts, producing a monotonous sequence that obscures critical distinctions between new and existing concepts.

ontology.one_sentence: A self-contained motion unit with one communication purpose.
ontology.essence: A self-contained motion unit with one communication purpose.
ontology.boundary: This is not the same as a generic implementation detail or unrelated concept.
ontology.trigger_signal: User input mentions 'scene' or describes this scenario.

ontology.essence: A time segment where one idea changes visually.

ontology.trigger_signal: User input mentions 'beat' or describes this scenario.

core_structure.from: attention path, timing, hierarchy, motion grammar, technical feasibility, review criteria

stances: Prefer composable scene components and named animation beats over clever but opaque code.
Use motion to reveal cause and effect, not just make diagrams move.
